abstract:
The k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm, a cornerstone of data science since 1951, remains a vital tool for data mining, recommendation engines, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Despite its longevity, traditional kNN faces significant performance bottlenecks when applied to the high-dimensional datasets characteristic of modern big data. This paper provides a comprehensive review and performance analysis of exact kNN modifications specifically designed to mitigate the "curse of dimensionality" in kNN Search and kNN Join operations.

We present an in-depth methodological assessment of 31 kNN search techniques and 12 kNN join methods, offering critical insights into their architectural strengths, inherent limitations, and practical use cases. A distinctive feature of this study is the inclusion of source code for the discussed methodologies, empowering researchers to engage in direct experimentation and comparative benchmarking. 

Bridging a recognized gap in the literature regarding exact (rather than approximate) high-dimensional kNN techniques, this survey serves as a holistic guide for researchers and practitioners. We conclude by identifying persistent challenges in the field and outlining future research trajectories, including the integration of ensemble learning and dynamic spatial partitioning to further enhance the scalability and precision of kNN in large-scale environments.
